description_long: This is a single-bladed axe with a robust, slightly curved handle that appears to be made of yellow-painted wood or composite material, with a black grip section at the bottom. The axe head is metallic, featuring a broad, wedge-shaped blade with a red-painted upper section and a sharp, silver cutting edge. The blade is securely mounted to the handle, with the mounting area showing a clear transition between the red and metallic surfaces. The handle shows signs of wear, with visible scuffs and marks, suggesting frequent use. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty tasks such as chopping wood or emergency use in urban environments. The object is compact and portable, making it ideal for storage in garages, maintenance rooms, or emergency kits.
description: A yellow-handled axe with a red and silver metal blade, featuring a black grip and visible wear marks. The design is practical and sturdy, suitable for urban utility or emergency use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the yellow handle, black grip, and the red and silver axe blade.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other face of the axe, with similar handle and blade features as view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the axe head and the full length of the handle, with the black grip visible.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved, yellow-painted handle with a black rubberized grip; likely wood or composite, oval cross-section, ergonomic for secure holding.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a straight edge, red-painted upper section, and a flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible transition between handle and blade.
